disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Three consecutive doses of the resident's regular Parkinson's medication missed over 12 hours because of new swallowing difficulty
trigger_question_examples: Has he been able to take his usual medicines today?
When did he last have his Parkinson's tablets?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's missed his last three lots of Parkinson's tablets over the last 12 hours because every time we try he chokes on them.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New acute dysphagia — choking on tablets and thin fluids, with saliva pooling in the mouth
trigger_question_examples: Is he coughing or choking when he tries to swallow?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — he's choking on his tablets and even on sips of water, and saliva is pooling in his mouth because he can't swallow it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute severe rigidity and inability to move ('locked up' / frozen) — a marked change from baseline
trigger_question_examples: How is he moving compared with normal?
Can he move his arms and legs at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's completely locked up — he can't move at all in the bed, and he's much stiffer than his usual stiff spells.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Violent resting tremor and very soft whispery voice, high distress
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His right hand is shaking violently and he can only whisper — he's very frightened.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negatives on the carer-observable respiratory screen — no fever, no wet/productive cough, breathing not rapid at present
trigger_question_examples: Does he have a fever or a wet cough?
Is he breathing fast or sounding chesty?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever on the thermometer, he's not coughing up anything and his breathing isn't fast at the moment — his oxygen reads 96%.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known background — advanced Parkinson's disease, Parkinson's dementia with visual hallucinations, recurrent backward falls, postural dizziness, chronic constipation
trigger_question_examples: Any history of falls or memory problems?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's had Parkinson's for years, he has the Parkinson's dementia and sees things sometimes, he falls backwards a lot, gets dizzy standing, and he's very constipated.
